data.name: economic-calendar-data-scraper
data.description: [💰 $8.0 / 1K] Extract economic calendar events from Investing.com — event name, country, date, time, importance, and actual/forecast/previous values. Filter by countries, importance, categories, and date range.
data.title: Economic Calendar Data Scraper

apify-actor-start.eventDescription: Charged when the Actor starts running. Number of events charged depends on Actor memory (one event per GB, minimum one event).

apify-default-dataset-item.eventDescription: Single result in the default dataset.

data.readmeSummary:
## Economic Calendar Data Scraper

Scrapes the Investing.com economic calendar at scale to produce structured, machine-readable macro-event data for trading and research workflows. The Actor returns one flat event row per release containing a localized date/time (or an "All Day" flag for undated items), country/region with ISO currency tagging, impact-tier label, full release name with reference period, unit-formatted actual/forecast/previous values for surprise/delta calculations, a source link to the original release, and a collection timestamp. Technical capabilities include filtering across ~45 economies, three importance tiers, and eight event categories (inflation, employment, central banks, economic activity, confidence index, credit, bonds, balance); historical and forward date-range queries; automatic timezone conversion across GMT-12:00 to GMT+13:00 (including half-hour offsets); multi-filter fan-out with automatic deduplication; and explicit handling of midnight/undated tentative releases. Keywords: Investing.com, economic calendar, macro releases, CPI, Non‑Farm Payrolls, central‑bank decisions, ISO currency, impact tiers, actual vs forecast vs previous, timezone localization, event stream, surprise/delta, backtesting, market-moving events.

## Use cases

- Algorithmic trading: schedule strategies around market‑moving releases, compute surprise/delta signals from actual vs forecast, hedge or halt positions around scheduled volatility, backtest signals against historical event windows.  
- Macro research: aggregate inflation and employment prints across ~45 economies, compare central‑bank actions and confidence indices, build event‑study models on forecast vs actual gaps, assemble longitudinal archives for trend analysis.  
- Risk management: map upcoming high‑impact events to exposed currency pairs, pre‑position alerts for the trading day, maintain rolling watchlists of countries and categories, quantify beat/miss frequency for releases.  
- Fintech & data products: power economic‑calendar widgets and dashboards, feed forecast/actual deltas into signal pipelines, localize event times per user/session, enrich charting tools with event markers linked to source releases.  
- Content & media: generate weekly macro previews, publish post‑release recaps comparing actuals to forecasts, and auto‑populate market‑calendar pages with structured release data.
